designations.designation: Resting heart rate
Over the past 30 days, heart rate measured at rest in beats per minute

definitions.definition: Number of cardiac cycles per minute measured in a resting state, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Systolic blood pressure
Over the past 30 days, peak arterial pressure during cardiac contraction

definitions.definition: Maximum arterial blood pressure during ventricular systole in mmHg, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Diastolic blood pressure
Over the past 30 days, minimum arterial pressure between heartbeats

definitions.definition: Lowest arterial blood pressure during ventricular diastole in mmHg, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Mean arterial pressure
Over the past 30 days, average arterial pressure over one cardiac cycle

definitions.definition: Weighted average blood pressure throughout one complete cardiac cycle, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Cardiac output
Over the past 30 days, volume of blood pumped by the heart per minute

definitions.definition: Total volume of blood ejected by the heart each minute in liters, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Left ventricular ejection fraction
Over the past 30 days, percentage of blood ejected from left ventricle per beat

definitions.definition: Proportion of left ventricular blood volume pumped per contraction, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: QT interval duration
Over the past 30 days, time from QRS complex onset to T-wave end

definitions.definition: Duration of ventricular depolarization and repolarization on ECG, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Troponin I concentration
Over the past 30 days, serum cardiac troponin I level

definitions.definition: Blood concentration of cardiac-specific troponin I protein marker, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: B-type natriuretic peptide level
Over the past 30 days, plasma BNP concentration

definitions.definition: Circulating plasma level of B-type natriuretic peptide hormone, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Total cholesterol
Over the past 30 days, total serum cholesterol concentration

definitions.definition: Sum of all cholesterol fractions measured in blood serum samples, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: LDL cholesterol
Over the past 30 days, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol level

definitions.definition: Serum concentration of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol fraction, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: HDL cholesterol
Over the past 30 days, high-density lipoprotein cholesterol level

definitions.definition: Serum concentration of high-density lipoprotein cholesterol fraction, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Triglyceride level
Over the past 30 days, fasting serum triglyceride concentration

definitions.definition: Fasting blood level of triglyceride lipids measured in serum, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: C-reactive protein
Over the past 30 days, high-sensitivity CRP concentration

definitions.definition: Serum level of high-sensitivity C-reactive protein inflammation marker, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Carotid intima-media thickness
Over the past 30 days, ultrasound measurement of carotid artery wall thickness

definitions.definition: Combined thickness of carotid artery intimal and medial layers, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Heart rate variability
Over the past 30 days, variation in time between consecutive heartbeats

definitions.definition: Standard deviation of time intervals between consecutive heartbeats, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Body mass index at cardiac assessment
Over the past 30 days, bMI calculated from height and weight at cardiac visit

definitions.definition: Weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared at visit, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Cardiac medication adherence
Over the past 30 days, self-reported adherence to prescribed cardiac medications

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance rate with prescribed cardiac drug regimen, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Age at cardiovascular screening
Over the past 30 days, patient age at time of cardiac risk evaluation

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of cardiovascular risk factor assessment, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Cardiac treatment response score
Over the past 30 days, overall response to cardiovascular treatment regimen

definitions.definition: Clinician-rated overall response to prescribed cardiac therapy, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Forced expiratory volume in one second
Over the past 30 days, maximum volume of air exhaled in the first second of a forced breath

definitions.definition: The volume of air that can be forcefully expelled from the lungs in the first second of a maximal expiratory effort after full inspiration. FEV1 is the primary measure of airflow limitation, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Forced vital capacity
Over the past 30 days, total volume of air exhaled during a complete forced expiration

definitions.definition: The maximum volume of air that can be forcefully exhaled from the lungs following a maximal inhalation. Forced vital capacity reflects total lung volume accessible during forced breathing, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: FEV1 to FVC ratio
Over the past 30 days, ratio of forced expiratory volume to forced vital capacity

definitions.definition: The proportion of forced vital capacity expelled in the first second of forced exhalation. A reduced ratio below the lower limit of normal indicates obstructive ventilatory impairment, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Peak expiratory flow rate
Over the past 30 days, maximum airflow speed achieved during forced exhalation

definitions.definition: The highest instantaneous flow rate attained during a maximal forced expiratory maneuver. Peak flow monitoring tracks day-to-day variability in airway caliber, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry
Over the past 30 days, peripheral blood oxygen saturation measured noninvasively

definitions.definition: The percentage of arterial hemoglobin saturated with oxygen as measured by pulse oximetry at the fingertip. Values below ninety percent typically indicate clinically significant hypoxemia, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Respiratory rate
Over the past 30 days, number of breaths taken per minute at rest

definitions.definition: The frequency of breathing cycles per minute recorded at rest. Normal adult respiratory rate ranges from twelve to twenty breaths per minute; deviations suggest respiratory compromise, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Arterial partial pressure of oxygen
Over the past 30 days, oxygen tension in arterial blood sample

definitions.definition: The partial pressure of dissolved oxygen in arterial blood measured from an arterial blood gas sample. PaO2 directly reflects the efficiency of pulmonary gas exchange, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide
Over the past 30 days, carbon dioxide tension in arterial blood sample

definitions.definition: The partial pressure of dissolved carbon dioxide in arterial blood. Elevated PaCO2 indicates hypoventilation or impaired carbon dioxide elimination by the respiratory system, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Six-minute walk distance
Over the past 30 days, total distance walked in six minutes on a flat corridor

definitions.definition: The maximum distance a patient can walk on a flat surface in six minutes under standardized conditions. The six-minute walk test assesses functional exercise capacity in cardiopulmonary disease, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Diffusing capacity for carbon monoxide
Over the past 30 days, rate of carbon monoxide transfer across the alveolar membrane

definitions.definition: The volume of carbon monoxide absorbed from inspired gas per unit time per unit pressure gradient. DLCO evaluates the integrity of the alveolar-capillary membrane for gas exchange, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Tidal volume
Over the past 30 days, volume of air inhaled or exhaled in a normal resting breath

definitions.definition: The volume of air moved into and out of the lungs during a single normal breathing cycle at rest. Tidal volume is a fundamental parameter in ventilatory mechanics assessment, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Bronchodilator response
Over the past 30 days, change in FEV1 after inhaled bronchodilator administration

definitions.definition: The percentage improvement in forced expiratory volume after administration of an inhaled short-acting bronchodilator. A significant response supports the diagnosis of reversible airway obstruction, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Dyspnea severity score
Over the past 30 days, patient-reported breathlessness severity rating

definitions.definition: A standardized numeric rating of the patient's perceived difficulty of breathing, assessed using a validated dyspnea scale. Higher scores indicate more severe breathlessness, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Exhaled nitric oxide concentration
Over the past 30 days, fractional concentration of nitric oxide in exhaled breath

definitions.definition: The level of nitric oxide measured in exhaled breath, expressed in parts per billion. Elevated exhaled nitric oxide is a marker of eosinophilic airway inflammation, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Chest radiograph classification
Over the past 30 days, diagnostic category assigned to the chest X-ray findings

definitions.definition: The radiologist's classification of chest radiograph findings into a diagnostic category. Chest imaging aids in the evaluation of pulmonary parenchymal and pleural abnormalities, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Blood pressure at pulmonary function visit
Over the past 30 days, resting blood pressure recorded at pulmonary clinic appointment

definitions.definition: Systolic and diastolic blood pressure measured in the seated position at the pulmonary function laboratory visit. Blood pressure is a routine vital sign during respiratory assessment, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Body mass index at respiratory assessment
Over the past 30 days, bMI at the time of pulmonary function evaluation

definitions.definition: Weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared recorded at the respiratory assessment visit. BMI influences lung volumes and respiratory mechanics in clinical evaluation, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Respiratory medication adherence
Over the past 30 days, self-reported adherence to prescribed respiratory medications

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance rate with prescribed inhaler and respiratory medication regimens. Adherence affects treatment outcomes and disease control in chronic respiratory conditions, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Age at pulmonary function evaluation
Over the past 30 days, patient age at the time of lung function testing

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of pulmonary function testing. Age is a key determinant of predicted reference values for spirometric and diffusion capacity measurements, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Pulmonary treatment response rating
Over the past 30 days, clinician-rated response to respiratory treatment plan

definitions.definition: Overall assessment of the patient's clinical response to the prescribed respiratory treatment regimen. Treatment response informs decisions on therapy continuation or adjustment, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Fasting blood glucose concentration
Over the past 30 days, blood sugar level measured after an overnight fast of at least eight hours used to screen for diabetes mellitus

definitions.definition: Fasting blood glucose is the concentration of glucose in venous plasma measured after an overnight fast of at least eight hours. The test is a primary screening tool for diabetes mellitus and prediabetes. Fasting glucose values of 100 to 125 milligrams per deciliter indicate impaired fasting glucose, while values at or above 126 milligrams per deciliter are diagnostic of diabetes when confirmed on repeat testing, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Hemoglobin A1c percentage
Over the past 30 days, glycated hemoglobin level reflecting average blood glucose over the preceding two to three months

definitions.definition: Hemoglobin A1c measures the percentage of hemoglobin molecules that have undergone non-enzymatic glycation, providing an integrated estimate of average blood glucose concentration over the preceding two to three month lifespan of circulating red blood cells. An A1c value of 6.5 percent or higher is diagnostic of diabetes mellitus, while values between 5.7 and 6.4 percent indicate increased risk for developing diabetes, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Fasting insulin level
Over the past 30 days, serum insulin concentration measured after overnight fasting used to evaluate insulin secretion and resistance

definitions.definition: Fasting serum insulin is the concentration of insulin in venous blood collected after an overnight fast. When interpreted alongside fasting glucose, elevated insulin levels suggest insulin resistance, a hallmark of metabolic syndrome and type 2 diabetes. Fasting insulin is also used to calculate surrogate indices of insulin resistance such as the homeostatic model assessment for insulin resistance, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: HOMA-IR insulin resistance index
Over the past 30 days, homeostatic model assessment score estimating insulin resistance from fasting glucose and fasting insulin values

definitions.definition: The homeostatic model assessment for insulin resistance is calculated as the product of fasting glucose in millimoles per liter and fasting insulin in microunits per milliliter divided by a constant of 22.5. Higher values indicate greater insulin resistance. HOMA-IR is widely used in epidemiological studies and clinical research as a practical surrogate measure of insulin sensitivity without requiring invasive dynamic tests, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Waist circumference measurement
Over the past 30 days, circumferential measurement of the abdomen at the level of the iliac crest used to assess central adiposity

definitions.definition: Waist circumference is measured at the level of the iliac crest using a flexible non-stretchable tape applied horizontally around the abdomen. The measurement, recorded in centimeters, serves as a clinical indicator of central adiposity and visceral fat accumulation. Elevated waist circumference is an independent risk factor for cardiovascular disease and is one of the diagnostic criteria for metabolic syndrome, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Body fat percentage by bioimpedance
Over the past 30 days, proportion of total body mass composed of adipose tissue estimated using bioelectrical impedance analysis

definitions.definition: Body fat percentage estimated by bioelectrical impedance analysis measures the proportion of total body weight attributable to fat mass. The method passes a low-level electrical current through the body and estimates fat-free mass based on the impedance of different tissue types. Although less precise than dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry, bioimpedance is non-invasive, portable, and suitable for repeated clinical monitoring of body composition changes, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Total cholesterol in metabolic panel
Over the past 30 days, total serum cholesterol concentration as part of the comprehensive metabolic lipid assessment

definitions.definition: Total cholesterol represents the sum of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, high-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and triglyceride-derived cholesterol fractions in a fasting blood sample. The measurement is reported in milligrams per deciliter and serves as an initial screening value for dyslipidemia. Desirable total cholesterol is below 200 milligrams per deciliter according to current clinical guidelines, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Triglyceride concentration in metabolic panel
Over the past 30 days, fasting serum triglyceride level measured as part of the metabolic lipid profile evaluation

definitions.definition: Serum triglycerides are measured in a fasting blood sample and reported in milligrams per deciliter. Elevated triglycerides are associated with increased cardiovascular risk and are a component of the metabolic syndrome diagnostic criteria. Triglyceride levels above 150 milligrams per deciliter are considered borderline elevated, while levels exceeding 500 milligrams per deciliter increase the risk of acute pancreatitis, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Uric acid level
Over the past 30 days, serum uric acid concentration reflecting purine metabolism and renal excretion balance

definitions.definition: Serum uric acid is the end product of purine metabolism and is measured in milligrams per deciliter. Elevated uric acid levels indicate either overproduction or underexcretion and are associated with gout, chronic kidney disease, and metabolic syndrome. Hyperuricemia is increasingly recognized as an independent risk factor for cardiovascular events and progression of renal impairment, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Alanine aminotransferase activity
Over the past 30 days, serum ALT enzyme level as a marker of hepatocellular injury in metabolic liver disease screening

definitions.definition: Alanine aminotransferase is a cytoplasmic enzyme concentrated in hepatocytes whose elevation in serum indicates hepatocellular damage. ALT activity is measured in units per liter and is commonly included in metabolic health panels to screen for non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. Persistent ALT elevation above the upper reference limit warrants further investigation for metabolic, viral, or toxic causes of liver injury, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Aspartate aminotransferase activity
Over the past 30 days, serum AST enzyme level as a marker of tissue injury used alongside ALT in metabolic liver screening

definitions.definition: Aspartate aminotransferase is present in hepatocytes, cardiac myocytes, and skeletal muscle cells. Serum AST is measured in units per liter and is interpreted alongside ALT to characterize the pattern and severity of liver injury. The AST to ALT ratio provides diagnostic information, with ratios above two suggesting alcoholic liver disease and ratios below one being more typical of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Serum creatinine concentration
Over the past 30 days, blood creatinine level as a marker of kidney filtration function in metabolic health assessment

definitions.definition: Serum creatinine is a breakdown product of creatine phosphate in skeletal muscle and is freely filtered by the glomeruli. The concentration, measured in milligrams per deciliter, inversely reflects the glomerular filtration rate. Serum creatinine is used to calculate estimated glomerular filtration rate and to stage chronic kidney disease, which frequently coexists with diabetes and metabolic syndrome, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Estimated glomerular filtration rate
Over the past 30 days, calculated measure of kidney filtration capacity derived from serum creatinine, age, sex, and race

definitions.definition: Estimated glomerular filtration rate is calculated from serum creatinine using validated equations such as the CKD-EPI formula that incorporate age, sex, and race. The result, expressed in milliliters per minute per 1.73 square meters of body surface area, classifies kidney function into stages of chronic kidney disease. An eGFR below 60 sustained for three months or more defines chronic kidney disease, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Thyroid-stimulating hormone level
Over the past 30 days, serum TSH concentration used to evaluate thyroid function in the context of metabolic health screening

definitions.definition: Thyroid-stimulating hormone is measured in milli-international units per liter and serves as the primary screening test for thyroid dysfunction. Hypothyroidism, indicated by elevated TSH, contributes to weight gain, dyslipidemia, and impaired glucose metabolism. Conversely, suppressed TSH suggests hyperthyroidism with associated metabolic acceleration. TSH screening is included in metabolic health panels due to the bidirectional relationship between thyroid function and metabolic status, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Abdominal ultrasound for hepatic steatosis
Over the past 30 days, ultrasound imaging of the liver to assess for fatty infiltration in the context of metabolic syndrome screening

definitions.definition: Abdominal ultrasound is a non-invasive imaging modality used to detect hepatic steatosis by evaluating liver echogenicity relative to the renal cortex. Increased liver echogenicity suggests fat infiltration and is graded as mild, moderate, or severe. Ultrasound screening for fatty liver is recommended in patients with obesity, insulin resistance, or elevated liver enzymes as part of comprehensive metabolic assessment, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Blood pressure at metabolic screening visit
Over the past 30 days, resting blood pressure recorded during the metabolic health assessment appointment to evaluate cardiovascular risk

definitions.definition: Blood pressure is measured in the seated position after five minutes of rest using an automated oscillometric device during the metabolic health screening visit. The measurement records both systolic and diastolic values in millimeters of mercury. Hypertension is a core component of metabolic syndrome and an independent cardiovascular risk factor that is routinely assessed alongside glycemic and lipid parameters, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Body mass index at metabolic assessment
Over the past 30 days, bMI calculated from measured height and weight at the metabolic health evaluation visit

definitions.definition: Body mass index is calculated by dividing body weight in kilograms by the square of height in meters and is recorded at the metabolic health assessment visit. BMI provides a standardized classification of weight status: underweight below 18.5, normal weight 18.5 to 24.9, overweight 25 to 29.9, and obese 30 or above. BMI is used as a screening criterion for metabolic syndrome and associated comorbidities, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Metabolic medication adherence
Over the past 30 days, self-reported adherence to prescribed medications for metabolic conditions including diabetes and dyslipidemia

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance with prescribed pharmacotherapy for metabolic conditions such as type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, and hypertension. Adherence is assessed using standardized questionnaires and expressed as a percentage of prescribed doses taken. Poor medication adherence is a major contributor to suboptimal glycemic control, persistent dyslipidemia, and increased cardiovascular event risk, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Age at metabolic health screening
Over the past 30 days, patient age at the time of the metabolic health assessment used as a covariate in cardiovascular risk estimation

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of the comprehensive metabolic health screening visit. Age is a non-modifiable risk factor for metabolic syndrome, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. Advancing age is associated with declining insulin sensitivity, increasing visceral adiposity, and progressive changes in lipid metabolism that collectively elevate cardiometabolic risk, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Metabolic treatment response score
Over the past 30 days, composite clinical assessment of response to metabolic intervention including lifestyle and pharmacological therapy

definitions.definition: A composite score reflecting the overall clinical response to metabolic interventions including dietary modification, exercise prescription, and pharmacotherapy. The score integrates changes in fasting glucose, hemoglobin A1c, lipid profile, blood pressure, and body weight from baseline to follow-up. Favorable treatment response is defined as clinically meaningful improvement in two or more metabolic parameters over the treatment period, measured over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Resting heart rate
Heart rate measured at rest in beats per minute

definitions.definition: Number of cardiac cycles per minute measured in a resting state. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Systolic blood pressure
Peak arterial pressure during cardiac contraction

definitions.definition: Maximum arterial blood pressure during ventricular systole in mmHg. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Diastolic blood pressure
Minimum arterial pressure between heartbeats

definitions.definition: Lowest arterial blood pressure during ventricular diastole in mmHg. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Mean arterial pressure
Average arterial pressure over one cardiac cycle

definitions.definition: Weighted average blood pressure throughout one complete cardiac cycle. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Cardiac output
Volume of blood pumped by the heart per minute

definitions.definition: Total volume of blood ejected by the heart each minute in liters. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Left ventricular ejection fraction
Percentage of blood ejected from left ventricle per beat

definitions.definition: Proportion of left ventricular blood volume pumped per contraction. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - QT interval duration
Time from QRS complex onset to T-wave end

definitions.definition: Duration of ventricular depolarization and repolarization on ECG. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Troponin I concentration
Serum cardiac troponin I level

definitions.definition: Blood concentration of cardiac-specific troponin I protein marker. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - B-type natriuretic peptide level
Plasma BNP concentration

definitions.definition: Circulating plasma level of B-type natriuretic peptide hormone. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Total cholesterol
Total serum cholesterol concentration

definitions.definition: Sum of all cholesterol fractions measured in blood serum samples. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - LDL cholesterol
Low-density lipoprotein cholesterol level

definitions.definition: Serum concentration of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol fraction. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - HDL cholesterol
High-density lipoprotein cholesterol level

definitions.definition: Serum concentration of high-density lipoprotein cholesterol fraction. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Triglyceride level
Fasting serum triglyceride concentration

definitions.definition: Fasting blood level of triglyceride lipids measured in serum. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - C-reactive protein
High-sensitivity CRP concentration

definitions.definition: Serum level of high-sensitivity C-reactive protein inflammation marker. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Carotid intima-media thickness
Ultrasound measurement of carotid artery wall thickness

definitions.definition: Combined thickness of carotid artery intimal and medial layers. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Heart rate variability
Variation in time between consecutive heartbeats

definitions.definition: Standard deviation of time intervals between consecutive heartbeats. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Body mass index at cardiac assessment
BMI calculated from height and weight at cardiac visit

definitions.definition: Weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared at visit. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Cardiac medication adherence
Self-reported adherence to prescribed cardiac medications

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance rate with prescribed cardiac drug regimen. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Age at cardiovascular screening
Patient age at time of cardiac risk evaluation

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of cardiovascular risk factor assessment. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Cardiac treatment response score
Overall response to cardiovascular treatment regimen

definitions.definition: Clinician-rated overall response to prescribed cardiac therapy. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Forced expiratory volume in one second
Maximum volume of air exhaled in the first second of a forced breath

definitions.definition: The volume of air that can be forcefully expelled from the lungs in the first second of a maximal expiratory effort after full inspiration. FEV1 is the primary measure of airflow limitation. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Forced vital capacity
Total volume of air exhaled during a complete forced expiration

definitions.definition: The maximum volume of air that can be forcefully exhaled from the lungs following a maximal inhalation. Forced vital capacity reflects total lung volume accessible during forced breathing. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - FEV1 to FVC ratio
Ratio of forced expiratory volume to forced vital capacity

definitions.definition: The proportion of forced vital capacity expelled in the first second of forced exhalation. A reduced ratio below the lower limit of normal indicates obstructive ventilatory impairment. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Peak expiratory flow rate
Maximum airflow speed achieved during forced exhalation

definitions.definition: The highest instantaneous flow rate attained during a maximal forced expiratory maneuver. Peak flow monitoring tracks day-to-day variability in airway caliber. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry
Peripheral blood oxygen saturation measured noninvasively

definitions.definition: The percentage of arterial hemoglobin saturated with oxygen as measured by pulse oximetry at the fingertip. Values below ninety percent typically indicate clinically significant hypoxemia. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Respiratory rate
Number of breaths taken per minute at rest

definitions.definition: The frequency of breathing cycles per minute recorded at rest. Normal adult respiratory rate ranges from twelve to twenty breaths per minute; deviations suggest respiratory compromise. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Arterial partial pressure of oxygen
Oxygen tension in arterial blood sample

definitions.definition: The partial pressure of dissolved oxygen in arterial blood measured from an arterial blood gas sample. PaO2 directly reflects the efficiency of pulmonary gas exchange. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide
Carbon dioxide tension in arterial blood sample

definitions.definition: The partial pressure of dissolved carbon dioxide in arterial blood. Elevated PaCO2 indicates hypoventilation or impaired carbon dioxide elimination by the respiratory system. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Six-minute walk distance
Total distance walked in six minutes on a flat corridor

definitions.definition: The maximum distance a patient can walk on a flat surface in six minutes under standardized conditions. The six-minute walk test assesses functional exercise capacity in cardiopulmonary disease. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Diffusing capacity for carbon monoxide
Rate of carbon monoxide transfer across the alveolar membrane

definitions.definition: The volume of carbon monoxide absorbed from inspired gas per unit time per unit pressure gradient. DLCO evaluates the integrity of the alveolar-capillary membrane for gas exchange. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Tidal volume
Volume of air inhaled or exhaled in a normal resting breath

definitions.definition: The volume of air moved into and out of the lungs during a single normal breathing cycle at rest. Tidal volume is a fundamental parameter in ventilatory mechanics assessment. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Bronchodilator response
Change in FEV1 after inhaled bronchodilator administration

definitions.definition: The percentage improvement in forced expiratory volume after administration of an inhaled short-acting bronchodilator. A significant response supports the diagnosis of reversible airway obstruction. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Dyspnea severity score
Patient-reported breathlessness severity rating

definitions.definition: A standardized numeric rating of the patient's perceived difficulty of breathing, assessed using a validated dyspnea scale. Higher scores indicate more severe breathlessness. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Exhaled nitric oxide concentration
Fractional concentration of nitric oxide in exhaled breath

definitions.definition: The level of nitric oxide measured in exhaled breath, expressed in parts per billion. Elevated exhaled nitric oxide is a marker of eosinophilic airway inflammation. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Chest radiograph classification
Diagnostic category assigned to the chest X-ray findings

definitions.definition: The radiologist's classification of chest radiograph findings into a diagnostic category. Chest imaging aids in the evaluation of pulmonary parenchymal and pleural abnormalities. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Blood pressure at pulmonary function visit
Resting blood pressure recorded at pulmonary clinic appointment

definitions.definition: Systolic and diastolic blood pressure measured in the seated position at the pulmonary function laboratory visit. Blood pressure is a routine vital sign during respiratory assessment. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Body mass index at respiratory assessment
BMI at the time of pulmonary function evaluation

definitions.definition: Weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared recorded at the respiratory assessment visit. BMI influences lung volumes and respiratory mechanics in clinical evaluation. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Respiratory medication adherence
Self-reported adherence to prescribed respiratory medications

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance rate with prescribed inhaler and respiratory medication regimens. Adherence affects treatment outcomes and disease control in chronic respiratory conditions. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Age at pulmonary function evaluation
Patient age at the time of lung function testing

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of pulmonary function testing. Age is a key determinant of predicted reference values for spirometric and diffusion capacity measurements. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Pulmonary treatment response rating
Clinician-rated response to respiratory treatment plan

definitions.definition: Overall assessment of the patient's clinical response to the prescribed respiratory treatment regimen. Treatment response informs decisions on therapy continuation or adjustment. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Fasting blood glucose concentration
Blood sugar level measured after an overnight fast of at least eight hours used to screen for diabetes mellitus

definitions.definition: Fasting blood glucose is the concentration of glucose in venous plasma measured after an overnight fast of at least eight hours. The test is a primary screening tool for diabetes mellitus and prediabetes. Fasting glucose values of 100 to 125 milligrams per deciliter indicate impaired fasting glucose, while values at or above 126 milligrams per deciliter are diagnostic of diabetes when confirmed on repeat testing. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Hemoglobin A1c percentage
Glycated hemoglobin level reflecting average blood glucose over the preceding two to three months

definitions.definition: Hemoglobin A1c measures the percentage of hemoglobin molecules that have undergone non-enzymatic glycation, providing an integrated estimate of average blood glucose concentration over the preceding two to three month lifespan of circulating red blood cells. An A1c value of 6.5 percent or higher is diagnostic of diabetes mellitus, while values between 5.7 and 6.4 percent indicate increased risk for developing diabetes. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Fasting insulin level
Serum insulin concentration measured after overnight fasting used to evaluate insulin secretion and resistance

definitions.definition: Fasting serum insulin is the concentration of insulin in venous blood collected after an overnight fast. When interpreted alongside fasting glucose, elevated insulin levels suggest insulin resistance, a hallmark of metabolic syndrome and type 2 diabetes. Fasting insulin is also used to calculate surrogate indices of insulin resistance such as the homeostatic model assessment for insulin resistance. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - HOMA-IR insulin resistance index
Homeostatic model assessment score estimating insulin resistance from fasting glucose and fasting insulin values

definitions.definition: The homeostatic model assessment for insulin resistance is calculated as the product of fasting glucose in millimoles per liter and fasting insulin in microunits per milliliter divided by a constant of 22.5. Higher values indicate greater insulin resistance. HOMA-IR is widely used in epidemiological studies and clinical research as a practical surrogate measure of insulin sensitivity without requiring invasive dynamic tests. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Waist circumference measurement
Circumferential measurement of the abdomen at the level of the iliac crest used to assess central adiposity

definitions.definition: Waist circumference is measured at the level of the iliac crest using a flexible non-stretchable tape applied horizontally around the abdomen. The measurement, recorded in centimeters, serves as a clinical indicator of central adiposity and visceral fat accumulation. Elevated waist circumference is an independent risk factor for cardiovascular disease and is one of the diagnostic criteria for metabolic syndrome. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Body fat percentage by bioimpedance
Proportion of total body mass composed of adipose tissue estimated using bioelectrical impedance analysis

definitions.definition: Body fat percentage estimated by bioelectrical impedance analysis measures the proportion of total body weight attributable to fat mass. The method passes a low-level electrical current through the body and estimates fat-free mass based on the impedance of different tissue types. Although less precise than dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry, bioimpedance is non-invasive, portable, and suitable for repeated clinical monitoring of body composition changes. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Total cholesterol in metabolic panel
Total serum cholesterol concentration as part of the comprehensive metabolic lipid assessment

definitions.definition: Total cholesterol represents the sum of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, high-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and triglyceride-derived cholesterol fractions in a fasting blood sample. The measurement is reported in milligrams per deciliter and serves as an initial screening value for dyslipidemia. Desirable total cholesterol is below 200 milligrams per deciliter according to current clinical guidelines. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Triglyceride concentration in metabolic panel
Fasting serum triglyceride level measured as part of the metabolic lipid profile evaluation

definitions.definition: Serum triglycerides are measured in a fasting blood sample and reported in milligrams per deciliter. Elevated triglycerides are associated with increased cardiovascular risk and are a component of the metabolic syndrome diagnostic criteria. Triglyceride levels above 150 milligrams per deciliter are considered borderline elevated, while levels exceeding 500 milligrams per deciliter increase the risk of acute pancreatitis. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Uric acid level
Serum uric acid concentration reflecting purine metabolism and renal excretion balance

definitions.definition: Serum uric acid is the end product of purine metabolism and is measured in milligrams per deciliter. Elevated uric acid levels indicate either overproduction or underexcretion and are associated with gout, chronic kidney disease, and metabolic syndrome. Hyperuricemia is increasingly recognized as an independent risk factor for cardiovascular events and progression of renal impairment. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Alanine aminotransferase activity
Serum ALT enzyme level as a marker of hepatocellular injury in metabolic liver disease screening

definitions.definition: Alanine aminotransferase is a cytoplasmic enzyme concentrated in hepatocytes whose elevation in serum indicates hepatocellular damage. ALT activity is measured in units per liter and is commonly included in metabolic health panels to screen for non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. Persistent ALT elevation above the upper reference limit warrants further investigation for metabolic, viral, or toxic causes of liver injury. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Aspartate aminotransferase activity
Serum AST enzyme level as a marker of tissue injury used alongside ALT in metabolic liver screening

definitions.definition: Aspartate aminotransferase is present in hepatocytes, cardiac myocytes, and skeletal muscle cells. Serum AST is measured in units per liter and is interpreted alongside ALT to characterize the pattern and severity of liver injury. The AST to ALT ratio provides diagnostic information, with ratios above two suggesting alcoholic liver disease and ratios below one being more typical of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Serum creatinine concentration
Blood creatinine level as a marker of kidney filtration function in metabolic health assessment

definitions.definition: Serum creatinine is a breakdown product of creatine phosphate in skeletal muscle and is freely filtered by the glomeruli. The concentration, measured in milligrams per deciliter, inversely reflects the glomerular filtration rate. Serum creatinine is used to calculate estimated glomerular filtration rate and to stage chronic kidney disease, which frequently coexists with diabetes and metabolic syndrome. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Estimated glomerular filtration rate
Calculated measure of kidney filtration capacity derived from serum creatinine, age, sex, and race

definitions.definition: Estimated glomerular filtration rate is calculated from serum creatinine using validated equations such as the CKD-EPI formula that incorporate age, sex, and race. The result, expressed in milliliters per minute per 1.73 square meters of body surface area, classifies kidney function into stages of chronic kidney disease. An eGFR below 60 sustained for three months or more defines chronic kidney disease. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Thyroid-stimulating hormone level
Serum TSH concentration used to evaluate thyroid function in the context of metabolic health screening

definitions.definition: Thyroid-stimulating hormone is measured in milli-international units per liter and serves as the primary screening test for thyroid dysfunction. Hypothyroidism, indicated by elevated TSH, contributes to weight gain, dyslipidemia, and impaired glucose metabolism. Conversely, suppressed TSH suggests hyperthyroidism with associated metabolic acceleration. TSH screening is included in metabolic health panels due to the bidirectional relationship between thyroid function and metabolic status. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Abdominal ultrasound for hepatic steatosis
Ultrasound imaging of the liver to assess for fatty infiltration in the context of metabolic syndrome screening

definitions.definition: Abdominal ultrasound is a non-invasive imaging modality used to detect hepatic steatosis by evaluating liver echogenicity relative to the renal cortex. Increased liver echogenicity suggests fat infiltration and is graded as mild, moderate, or severe. Ultrasound screening for fatty liver is recommended in patients with obesity, insulin resistance, or elevated liver enzymes as part of comprehensive metabolic assessment. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Blood pressure at metabolic screening visit
Resting blood pressure recorded during the metabolic health assessment appointment to evaluate cardiovascular risk

definitions.definition: Blood pressure is measured in the seated position after five minutes of rest using an automated oscillometric device during the metabolic health screening visit. The measurement records both systolic and diastolic values in millimeters of mercury. Hypertension is a core component of metabolic syndrome and an independent cardiovascular risk factor that is routinely assessed alongside glycemic and lipid parameters. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Body mass index at metabolic assessment
BMI calculated from measured height and weight at the metabolic health evaluation visit

definitions.definition: Body mass index is calculated by dividing body weight in kilograms by the square of height in meters and is recorded at the metabolic health assessment visit. BMI provides a standardized classification of weight status: underweight below 18.5, normal weight 18.5 to 24.9, overweight 25 to 29.9, and obese 30 or above. BMI is used as a screening criterion for metabolic syndrome and associated comorbidities. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Metabolic medication adherence
Self-reported adherence to prescribed medications for metabolic conditions including diabetes and dyslipidemia

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance with prescribed pharmacotherapy for metabolic conditions such as type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, and hypertension. Adherence is assessed using standardized questionnaires and expressed as a percentage of prescribed doses taken. Poor medication adherence is a major contributor to suboptimal glycemic control, persistent dyslipidemia, and increased cardiovascular event risk. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Age at metabolic health screening
Patient age at the time of the metabolic health assessment used as a covariate in cardiovascular risk estimation

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of the comprehensive metabolic health screening visit. Age is a non-modifiable risk factor for metabolic syndrome, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. Advancing age is associated with declining insulin sensitivity, increasing visceral adiposity, and progressive changes in lipid metabolism that collectively elevate cardiometabolic risk. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Metabolic treatment response score
Composite clinical assessment of response to metabolic intervention including lifestyle and pharmacological therapy

definitions.definition: A composite score reflecting the overall clinical response to metabolic interventions including dietary modification, exercise prescription, and pharmacotherapy. The score integrates changes in fasting glucose, hemoglobin A1c, lipid profile, blood pressure, and body weight from baseline to follow-up. Favorable treatment response is defined as clinically meaningful improvement in two or more metabolic parameters over the treatment period. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Resting heart rate
Over the past 30 days, heart rate measured at rest in beats per minute

definitions.definition: Number of cardiac cycles per minute measured in a resting state, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Systolic blood pressure
Over the past 30 days, peak arterial pressure during cardiac contraction

definitions.definition: Maximum arterial blood pressure during ventricular systole in mmHg, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Diastolic blood pressure
Over the past 30 days, minimum arterial pressure between heartbeats

definitions.definition: Lowest arterial blood pressure during ventricular diastole in mmHg, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Mean arterial pressure
Over the past 30 days, average arterial pressure over one cardiac cycle

definitions.definition: Weighted average blood pressure throughout one complete cardiac cycle, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Cardiac output
Over the past 30 days, volume of blood pumped by the heart per minute

definitions.definition: Total volume of blood ejected by the heart each minute in liters, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Left ventricular ejection fraction
Over the past 30 days, percentage of blood ejected from left ventricle per beat

definitions.definition: Proportion of left ventricular blood volume pumped per contraction, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - QT interval duration
Over the past 30 days, time from QRS complex onset to T-wave end

definitions.definition: Duration of ventricular depolarization and repolarization on ECG, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Troponin I concentration
Over the past 30 days, serum cardiac troponin I level

definitions.definition: Blood concentration of cardiac-specific troponin I protein marker, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - B-type natriuretic peptide level
Over the past 30 days, plasma BNP concentration

definitions.definition: Circulating plasma level of B-type natriuretic peptide hormone, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Total cholesterol
Over the past 30 days, total serum cholesterol concentration

definitions.definition: Sum of all cholesterol fractions measured in blood serum samples, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - LDL cholesterol
Over the past 30 days, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol level

definitions.definition: Serum concentration of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol fraction, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - HDL cholesterol
Over the past 30 days, high-density lipoprotein cholesterol level

definitions.definition: Serum concentration of high-density lipoprotein cholesterol fraction, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Triglyceride level
Over the past 30 days, fasting serum triglyceride concentration

definitions.definition: Fasting blood level of triglyceride lipids measured in serum, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - C-reactive protein
Over the past 30 days, high-sensitivity CRP concentration

definitions.definition: Serum level of high-sensitivity C-reactive protein inflammation marker, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Carotid intima-media thickness
Over the past 30 days, ultrasound measurement of carotid artery wall thickness

definitions.definition: Combined thickness of carotid artery intimal and medial layers, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Heart rate variability
Over the past 30 days, variation in time between consecutive heartbeats

definitions.definition: Standard deviation of time intervals between consecutive heartbeats, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Body mass index at cardiac assessment
Over the past 30 days, bMI calculated from height and weight at cardiac visit

definitions.definition: Weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared at visit, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Cardiac medication adherence
Over the past 30 days, self-reported adherence to prescribed cardiac medications

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance rate with prescribed cardiac drug regimen, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Age at cardiovascular screening
Over the past 30 days, patient age at time of cardiac risk evaluation

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of cardiovascular risk factor assessment, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Cardiac Assessment - Cardiac treatment response score
Over the past 30 days, overall response to cardiovascular treatment regimen

definitions.definition: Clinician-rated overall response to prescribed cardiac therapy, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Forced expiratory volume in one second
Over the past 30 days, maximum volume of air exhaled in the first second of a forced breath

definitions.definition: The volume of air that can be forcefully expelled from the lungs in the first second of a maximal expiratory effort after full inspiration. FEV1 is the primary measure of airflow limitation, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Forced vital capacity
Over the past 30 days, total volume of air exhaled during a complete forced expiration

definitions.definition: The maximum volume of air that can be forcefully exhaled from the lungs following a maximal inhalation. Forced vital capacity reflects total lung volume accessible during forced breathing, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - FEV1 to FVC ratio
Over the past 30 days, ratio of forced expiratory volume to forced vital capacity

definitions.definition: The proportion of forced vital capacity expelled in the first second of forced exhalation. A reduced ratio below the lower limit of normal indicates obstructive ventilatory impairment, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Peak expiratory flow rate
Over the past 30 days, maximum airflow speed achieved during forced exhalation

definitions.definition: The highest instantaneous flow rate attained during a maximal forced expiratory maneuver. Peak flow monitoring tracks day-to-day variability in airway caliber, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry
Over the past 30 days, peripheral blood oxygen saturation measured noninvasively

definitions.definition: The percentage of arterial hemoglobin saturated with oxygen as measured by pulse oximetry at the fingertip. Values below ninety percent typically indicate clinically significant hypoxemia, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Respiratory rate
Over the past 30 days, number of breaths taken per minute at rest

definitions.definition: The frequency of breathing cycles per minute recorded at rest. Normal adult respiratory rate ranges from twelve to twenty breaths per minute; deviations suggest respiratory compromise, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Arterial partial pressure of oxygen
Over the past 30 days, oxygen tension in arterial blood sample

definitions.definition: The partial pressure of dissolved oxygen in arterial blood measured from an arterial blood gas sample. PaO2 directly reflects the efficiency of pulmonary gas exchange, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Arterial partial pressure of carbon dioxide
Over the past 30 days, carbon dioxide tension in arterial blood sample

definitions.definition: The partial pressure of dissolved carbon dioxide in arterial blood. Elevated PaCO2 indicates hypoventilation or impaired carbon dioxide elimination by the respiratory system, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Six-minute walk distance
Over the past 30 days, total distance walked in six minutes on a flat corridor

definitions.definition: The maximum distance a patient can walk on a flat surface in six minutes under standardized conditions. The six-minute walk test assesses functional exercise capacity in cardiopulmonary disease, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Diffusing capacity for carbon monoxide
Over the past 30 days, rate of carbon monoxide transfer across the alveolar membrane

definitions.definition: The volume of carbon monoxide absorbed from inspired gas per unit time per unit pressure gradient. DLCO evaluates the integrity of the alveolar-capillary membrane for gas exchange, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Tidal volume
Over the past 30 days, volume of air inhaled or exhaled in a normal resting breath

definitions.definition: The volume of air moved into and out of the lungs during a single normal breathing cycle at rest. Tidal volume is a fundamental parameter in ventilatory mechanics assessment, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Bronchodilator response
Over the past 30 days, change in FEV1 after inhaled bronchodilator administration

definitions.definition: The percentage improvement in forced expiratory volume after administration of an inhaled short-acting bronchodilator. A significant response supports the diagnosis of reversible airway obstruction, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Dyspnea severity score
Over the past 30 days, patient-reported breathlessness severity rating

definitions.definition: A standardized numeric rating of the patient's perceived difficulty of breathing, assessed using a validated dyspnea scale. Higher scores indicate more severe breathlessness, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Exhaled nitric oxide concentration
Over the past 30 days, fractional concentration of nitric oxide in exhaled breath

definitions.definition: The level of nitric oxide measured in exhaled breath, expressed in parts per billion. Elevated exhaled nitric oxide is a marker of eosinophilic airway inflammation, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Chest radiograph classification
Over the past 30 days, diagnostic category assigned to the chest X-ray findings

definitions.definition: The radiologist's classification of chest radiograph findings into a diagnostic category. Chest imaging aids in the evaluation of pulmonary parenchymal and pleural abnormalities, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Blood pressure at pulmonary function visit
Over the past 30 days, resting blood pressure recorded at pulmonary clinic appointment

definitions.definition: Systolic and diastolic blood pressure measured in the seated position at the pulmonary function laboratory visit. Blood pressure is a routine vital sign during respiratory assessment, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Body mass index at respiratory assessment
Over the past 30 days, bMI at the time of pulmonary function evaluation

definitions.definition: Weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared recorded at the respiratory assessment visit. BMI influences lung volumes and respiratory mechanics in clinical evaluation, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Respiratory medication adherence
Over the past 30 days, self-reported adherence to prescribed respiratory medications

definitions.definition: Patient-reported compliance rate with prescribed inhaler and respiratory medication regimens. Adherence affects treatment outcomes and disease control in chronic respiratory conditions, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Age at pulmonary function evaluation
Over the past 30 days, patient age at the time of lung function testing

definitions.definition: Age in years at the time of pulmonary function testing. Age is a key determinant of predicted reference values for spirometric and diffusion capacity measurements, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Pulmonary Evaluation - Pulmonary treatment response rating
Over the past 30 days, clinician-rated response to respiratory treatment plan

definitions.definition: Overall assessment of the patient's clinical response to the prescribed respiratory treatment regimen. Treatment response informs decisions on therapy continuation or adjustment, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Fasting blood glucose concentration
Over the past 30 days, blood sugar level measured after an overnight fast of at least eight hours used to screen for diabetes mellitus

definitions.definition: Fasting blood glucose is the concentration of glucose in venous plasma measured after an overnight fast of at least eight hours. The test is a primary screening tool for diabetes mellitus and prediabetes. Fasting glucose values of 100 to 125 milligrams per deciliter indicate impaired fasting glucose, while values at or above 126 milligrams per deciliter are diagnostic of diabetes when confirmed on repeat testing, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Hemoglobin A1c percentage
Over the past 30 days, glycated hemoglobin level reflecting average blood glucose over the preceding two to three months

definitions.definition: Hemoglobin A1c measures the percentage of hemoglobin molecules that have undergone non-enzymatic glycation, providing an integrated estimate of average blood glucose concentration over the preceding two to three month lifespan of circulating red blood cells. An A1c value of 6.5 percent or higher is diagnostic of diabetes mellitus, while values between 5.7 and 6.4 percent indicate increased risk for developing diabetes, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Fasting insulin level
Over the past 30 days, serum insulin concentration measured after overnight fasting used to evaluate insulin secretion and resistance

definitions.definition: Fasting serum insulin is the concentration of insulin in venous blood collected after an overnight fast. When interpreted alongside fasting glucose, elevated insulin levels suggest insulin resistance, a hallmark of metabolic syndrome and type 2 diabetes. Fasting insulin is also used to calculate surrogate indices of insulin resistance such as the homeostatic model assessment for insulin resistance, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - HOMA-IR insulin resistance index
Over the past 30 days, homeostatic model assessment score estimating insulin resistance from fasting glucose and fasting insulin values

definitions.definition: The homeostatic model assessment for insulin resistance is calculated as the product of fasting glucose in millimoles per liter and fasting insulin in microunits per milliliter divided by a constant of 22.5. Higher values indicate greater insulin resistance. HOMA-IR is widely used in epidemiological studies and clinical research as a practical surrogate measure of insulin sensitivity without requiring invasive dynamic tests, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Waist circumference measurement
Over the past 30 days, circumferential measurement of the abdomen at the level of the iliac crest used to assess central adiposity

definitions.definition: Waist circumference is measured at the level of the iliac crest using a flexible non-stretchable tape applied horizontally around the abdomen. The measurement, recorded in centimeters, serves as a clinical indicator of central adiposity and visceral fat accumulation. Elevated waist circumference is an independent risk factor for cardiovascular disease and is one of the diagnostic criteria for metabolic syndrome, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Body fat percentage by bioimpedance
Over the past 30 days, proportion of total body mass composed of adipose tissue estimated using bioelectrical impedance analysis

definitions.definition: Body fat percentage estimated by bioelectrical impedance analysis measures the proportion of total body weight attributable to fat mass. The method passes a low-level electrical current through the body and estimates fat-free mass based on the impedance of different tissue types. Although less precise than dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry, bioimpedance is non-invasive, portable, and suitable for repeated clinical monitoring of body composition changes, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Total cholesterol in metabolic panel
Over the past 30 days, total serum cholesterol concentration as part of the comprehensive metabolic lipid assessment

definitions.definition: Total cholesterol represents the sum of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, high-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and triglyceride-derived cholesterol fractions in a fasting blood sample. The measurement is reported in milligrams per deciliter and serves as an initial screening value for dyslipidemia. Desirable total cholesterol is below 200 milligrams per deciliter according to current clinical guidelines, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Triglyceride concentration in metabolic panel
Over the past 30 days, fasting serum triglyceride level measured as part of the metabolic lipid profile evaluation

definitions.definition: Serum triglycerides are measured in a fasting blood sample and reported in milligrams per deciliter. Elevated triglycerides are associated with increased cardiovascular risk and are a component of the metabolic syndrome diagnostic criteria. Triglyceride levels above 150 milligrams per deciliter are considered borderline elevated, while levels exceeding 500 milligrams per deciliter increase the risk of acute pancreatitis, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Uric acid level
Over the past 30 days, serum uric acid concentration reflecting purine metabolism and renal excretion balance

definitions.definition: Serum uric acid is the end product of purine metabolism and is measured in milligrams per deciliter. Elevated uric acid levels indicate either overproduction or underexcretion and are associated with gout, chronic kidney disease, and metabolic syndrome. Hyperuricemia is increasingly recognized as an independent risk factor for cardiovascular events and progression of renal impairment, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Alanine aminotransferase activity
Over the past 30 days, serum ALT enzyme level as a marker of hepatocellular injury in metabolic liver disease screening

definitions.definition: Alanine aminotransferase is a cytoplasmic enzyme concentrated in hepatocytes whose elevation in serum indicates hepatocellular damage. ALT activity is measured in units per liter and is commonly included in metabolic health panels to screen for non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. Persistent ALT elevation above the upper reference limit warrants further investigation for metabolic, viral, or toxic causes of liver injury, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Aspartate aminotransferase activity
Over the past 30 days, serum AST enzyme level as a marker of tissue injury used alongside ALT in metabolic liver screening

definitions.definition: Aspartate aminotransferase is present in hepatocytes, cardiac myocytes, and skeletal muscle cells. Serum AST is measured in units per liter and is interpreted alongside ALT to characterize the pattern and severity of liver injury. The AST to ALT ratio provides diagnostic information, with ratios above two suggesting alcoholic liver disease and ratios below one being more typical of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Serum creatinine concentration
Over the past 30 days, blood creatinine level as a marker of kidney filtration function in metabolic health assessment

definitions.definition: Serum creatinine is a breakdown product of creatine phosphate in skeletal muscle and is freely filtered by the glomeruli. The concentration, measured in milligrams per deciliter, inversely reflects the glomerular filtration rate. Serum creatinine is used to calculate estimated glomerular filtration rate and to stage chronic kidney disease, which frequently coexists with diabetes and metabolic syndrome, measured over the past 30 days. A field of the Clinical Monitoring Protocol (CMP). Based on the Health Data Quality Framework (HDQF).

designations.designation: CMP Metabolic Panel - Estimated glomerular filtration rate
Over the past 30 days, calculated measure of kidney filtration capacity derived from serum creatinine, age, sex, and race

definitions.definition: Estimated glomerular filtration rate is calculated from serum creatinine using validated equations such as the CKD-EPI formula that incorporate age, sex, and race. The result, expressed in milliliters per minute per 1.73 square meters of body surface area, classifies kidney function into stages of chronic kidney disease.
